meaning: n.(古罗马社会中的)最下层阶级,工人阶级,尤指无产阶级
examples: The proletariat was then still a "class-in-itself".
He also recognized the "only the future but the emerging proletariat".
But, as a wise central banker, you know that for a country to grow its economy, it needs a singular currency so the proletariat doesn't get confused.
Government housing finance groups and Mexican commercial banks have begun offering mortgages in recent years, enabling more of the proletariat to own homes.
The bill to ban foxhunting has (at least in the eyes of the Labour left-wingers who promoted it) set the urban proletariat against the rural landlords.
